primary_accounting_issue: Appréciation du critère SPPI (« prêt basique ») en IFRS 9 pour un actif financier dont des termes contractuels peuvent modifier le calendrier ou le montant des flux de trésorerie, notamment en cas d’événement déclencheur.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the precedence rule strictly and selected IFRS 9 because it is the only IFRS standard in the context that directly governs classification of financial assets and the SPPI test; no overlapping IAS standard in the context governs this same classification issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is directly about SPPI under IFRS 9, and the cited IFRS 9 paragraphs explicitly address contingent or changing contractual cash flows.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern classification outcomes and make SPPI a core condition for amortised cost and FVOCI.
These paragraphs directly define the basic lending arrangement concept used in the SPPI analysis.
These paragraphs directly address contractual terms that can change the timing or amount of cash flows and when such features can or cannot still satisfy SPPI.

supporting_authority.reason: This paragraph adds the distinct classification possibility of FVTPL by designation despite the default classification logic.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address insurance contract cash flows and discounting, not IFRS 9 SPPI classification of financial assets.
These paragraphs concern hedge accounting rather than the SPPI classification test for financial assets.
These paragraphs concern inventories and financing elements in purchase arrangements, not the SPPI test under IFRS 9.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with SPPI cash flows held in a hold-to-collect business model
Financial assets with SPPI cash flows held in a hold-to-collect-and-sell business model
Financial assets not qualifying for the SPPI-based categories or otherwise classified under the residual category

approaches.label: Amortised Cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats amortised cost as a top-level measurement category when the asset has SPPI cash flows and is held to collect contractual cash flows.

approaches.label: Fair Value through Other Comprehensive Income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats FVOCI as a top-level measurement category when the asset has SPPI cash flows and is managed both to collect and to sell.

approaches.label: Fair Value through Profit or Loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats FVTPL as the residual top-level measurement category, including where contractual features cause the cash flows not to be solely payments of principal and interest.
